channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Security Affairs newsletter Round 565 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
item.description: A new round of the weekly Security Affairs newsletter has arrived! Every week, the best security articles from Security Affairs are free in your email box. Enjoy a new round of the weekly SecurityAffairs newsletter, i...

item.title: ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
item.description: OpenClaw has fixed a high-severity security issue that, if successfully exploited, could have allowed a malicious website to connect to a locally running artificial intelligence (AI) agent and take over control. "Our...

item.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
item.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control...

item.title: Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million Accounts
item.description: Names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and encrypted passwords were compromised in the attack. The post Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Thousands of Public Google Cloud API Keys Exposed with Gemini Access After API Enablement
item.description: New research has found that Google Cloud API keys, typically designated as project identifiers for billing purposes, could be abused to authenticate to sensitive Gemini endpoints and access private data. The findings...

item.title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic Supply Chain Risk Over AI Military Dispute
item.description: Anthropic on Friday hit back after U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth directed the Pentagon to designate the artificial intelligence (AI) upstart as a "supply chain risk." "This action follows months of negotiatio...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Fishing in Peru
item.description: Peru has increased its squid catch limit. The article says “giant squid,” but they can’t possibly mean that. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t cov...

item.title: Trump Orders All Federal Agencies to Phase Out Use of Anthropic Technology
item.description: OpenAI and Google, along with Elon Musk’s xAI, also have contracts to supply their AI models to the military. The post Trump Orders All Federal Agencies to Phase Out Use of Anthropic Technology appeared first on Secur...

item.title: 900+ Sangoma FreePBX Instances Compromised in Ongoing Web Shell Attacks
item.description: The Shadowserver Foundation has revealed that over 900 Sangoma FreePBX instances still remain infected with web shells as part of attacks that exploited a command injection vulnerability starting in December 2025. Of...

item.title: In Other News: ATT&CK Advisory Council, Russian Cyberattacks Aid Missile Strikes, Predator Bypasses iOS Indicators
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: cyber valuations surge, OpenAI disrupts malicious AI use, ShinyHunters claims Odido breach. The post In Other News: ATT&CK Advisory Council, Russian Cy...

item.title: Third-Party Patching and the Business Footprint We All Share
item.description: Everyday tools like PDF readers, email clients, and archive utilities quietly define the real attack surface. Action1 explains how third-party software drift increases exploit risk and why consistent patching reduces...

item.title: IronCurtain: An open-source, safeguard layer for autonomous AI assistants
item.description: Veteran security engineer Niels Provos is working on a new technical approach designed to stop autonomous AI agents from taking actions you haven’t specifically authorized. His open-source software solution, called Ir...

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Expands Toolkit to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The security researchers from Zscaler ThreatLabz have also discovered five new tools deployed by the North Korean hacking group

item.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
item.description: Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.

item.title: Meta tightens grip on scam advertisers
item.description: Meta is stepping up the fight against scams on its platforms by filing multiple lawsuits targeting companies and individuals in Brazil, China, and Vietnam who used deceptive tactics to run scam ads. The company said i...

item.title: 38 Million Allegedly Impacted by ManoMano Data Breach
item.description: Hackers stole personal information such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, and other information. The post 38 Million Allegedly Impacted by ManoMano Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: 900 Sangoma FreePBX Instances Infected With Web Shells
item.description: The attacks exploited a post-authentication command injection vulnerability in the endpoint manager’s interface. The post 900 Sangoma FreePBX Instances Infected With Web Shells appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: ScarCruft Uses Zoho WorkDrive and USB Malware to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as ScarCruft has been attributed to a fresh set of tools, including a backdoor that uses Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control (C2) communications to fetch more payloads and an imp...

item.title: NATO greenlights iPhone and iPad for classified information handling
item.description: Apple confirmed that the iPhone and iPad have been approved for use with classified information in NATO restricted environments. The devices will no longer require special software or settings to handle NATO restricte...

item.title: How AI Aids Incident Response: Why Humans Alone Cannot Do IR Efficiently
item.description: AI accelerates incident response by correlating alerts and generating reports in minutes, helping teams scale beyond manual limits. Incident response has always been a race against the clock. It starts ticking the mom...

item.title: 12 Million exposed .env files reveal widespread security failures
item.description: Mysterium VPN found 12M IPs exposing .env files, leaking credentials and revealing widespread security misconfigurations worldwide. Configuration mistakes rarely trigger alarms. A forgotten deny rule, an overlooked se...

item.title: Meta Files Lawsuits Against Brazil, China, Vietnam Advertisers Over Celeb-Bait Scams
item.description: Meta on Thursday said it's taking legal action to tackle scams on its platforms by filing lawsuits against what it calls deceptive advertisers based in Brazil, China, and Vietnam. As part of the effort, the advertiser...

item.title: ManoMano data breach impacted 38 Million customer accounts
item.description: European DIY platform ManoMano suffered a data breach via a third-party provider, exposing personal data of 38 million customers. European DIY e-commerce platform ManoMano disclosed a major data breach affecting 38 mi...

item.title: Critical Flaws Exposed Gardyn Smart Gardens to Remote Hacking
item.description: CISA has released an advisory to warn about four vulnerabilities discovered by a researcher in Gardyn Home and Gardyn Studio. The post Critical Flaws Exposed Gardyn Smart Gardens to Remote Hacking appeared first on Se...

item.title: Oculeus 2FN authenticates calls in real time to stop CLI spoofing
item.description: Oculeus has launched its new Two Factor Network (2FN) solution. The 2FN solution provides a framework for telcos to trace the origin of traffic, verify caller identity, determine the roaming status of inbound calls, a...

item.title: Bringing more transparency to post-quantum usage, encrypted messaging, and routing security
item.description: Cloudflare Radar has added new tools for monitoring PQ adoption, KT logs for messaging, and ASPA routing records to track the Internet's migration toward more secure encryption and routing standards.

item.title: ASPA: making Internet routing more secure
item.description: ASPA is the cryptographic upgrade for BGP that helps prevent route leaks by verifying the path network traffic takes. New features in Cloudflare Radar make tracking its adoption easy.

item.title: Previously harmless Google API keys now expose Gemini AI data
item.description: Google API keys for services like Maps embedded in accessible client-side code could be used to authenticate to the Gemini AI assistant and access private data. [...]

item.title: Aeternum C2 Botnet Stores Encrypted Commands on Polygon Blockchain to Evade Takedown
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new botnet loader called Aeternum C2 that uses a blockchain-based command-and-control (C2) infrastructure to make it resilient to takedown efforts. "Instead of rel...

item.title: Darktrace Flags 32 Million Phishing Emails in 2025 as Identity Attacks Intensify
item.description: 2025 saw 32M phishing emails, with identity threats surpassing vulnerabilities

item.title: Ransomware payment rate drops to record low as attacks surge
item.description: The number of ransomware victims paying threat actors has dropped to 28% last year, an all-time low, despite a significant increase in the number of claimed attacks. [...]

item.title: Exploitable Vulnerabilities Present in 87% of Organizations
item.description: Datadog report reveals two-fifths of services are affected by exploitable bugs

item.title: Finding Signal in the Noise: Lessons Learned Running a Honeypot with AI Assistance [Guest Diary], (Tue, Feb 24th)
item.description: [This is a Guest Diary by Austin Bodolay, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

item.title: Expert Recommends: Prepare for PQC Right Now
item.description: Introduction: Steal It Today, Break It in a Decade Digital evolution is unstoppable, and though the pace may vary, things tend to fall into place sooner rather than later. That, of course, applies to adversaries as we...

item.title: Global Cyber Agencies Urge Immediate Patching of Cisco SD-WAN Zero Day
item.description: The US and allies are urging Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN customers to hunt for signs of exploitation

item.title: CrowdStrike FalconID Brings Phishing-Resistant MFA to Falcon Next-Gen Identity Security
SLH Offers $500–$1,000 Per Call to Recruit Women for IT Help Desk Vishing Attacks
item.description: The notorious cybercrime collective known as Scattered LAPSUS$ Hunters (SLH) has been observed offering financial incentives to recruit women to pull off social engineering attacks. The idea is to hire them for voice...

item.title: 44% Surge in App Exploits as AI Speeds Up Cyber-Attacks, IBM Finds
item.description: IBM's 2026 X-Force report reveals 44% rise in cyber-attacks on public apps, driven by AI and flaws

item.title: Malicious NuGet Packages Stole ASP.NET Data; npm Package Dropped Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered four malicious NuGet packages that are designed to target ASP.NET web application developers to steal sensitive data. The campaign, discovered by Socket, exfiltrates ASP.NET I...

item.title: Defense Contractor Employee Jailed for Selling 8 Zero-Days to Russian Broker
item.description: A 39-year-old Australian national who was previously employed at U.S. defense contractor L3Harris has been sentenced to a little over seven years in prison for selling eight zero-day exploits to Russian exploit broker...

item.title: Operation Red Card 2.0 Leads to 651 Arrests in Africa
item.description: In the latest operation targeting cybercrime groups, African law enforcement agencies cooperated with Interpol and cybersecurity firms to recover more than $4.3 million.

item.title: SolarWinds Patches 4 Critical Serv-U 15.5 Flaws Allowing Root Code Execution
item.description: SolarWinds has released updates to address four critical security flaws in its Serv-U file transfer software that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution. The vulnerabilities, all rated 9.1 o...

item.title: How we rebuilt Next.js with AI in one week
item.description: One engineer used AI to rebuild Next.js on Vite in a week. vinext builds up to 4x faster, produces 57% smaller bundles, and deploys to Cloudflare Workers with a single command.

item.title: RoguePilot Flaw in GitHub Codespaces Enabled Copilot to Leak GITHUB_TOKEN
item.description: A vulnerability in GitHub Codespaces could have been exploited by bad actors to seize control of repositories by injecting malicious Copilot instructions in a GitHub issue. The artificial intelligence (AI)-driven vuln...

item.title: North Korean Lazarus Group Expands Ransomware Activity With Medusa
item.description: Ransomware Medusa linked to North Korean hackers targets US healthcare amid ongoing attacks

item.title: Bring the Fight to the Edge: Turning Time Into an Advantage in OT Security
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals most OT attacks begin in IT. Learn how edge-driven defense stops threats early and turns dwell time into advantage. The post Bring the Fight to the Edge: Turning Time Into an Advantage in OT S...

item.title: As Cybersecurity Firms Chase AI, VC Market Skyrockets
item.description: Investments in cybersecurity startups took off in 2025 as venture capital firms focused not just on AI-native tech but on talent as well.

item.title: AI Accelerates Attacker Breakout Time to Just Four Minutes
item.description: ReliaQuest claims AI has reduced breakout and exfiltration time to under 10 minutes

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-25108 Soliton Systems K.K. FileZen OS Command Injection Vulnerability This...

item.title: Chinese AI Firms Hit Claude with Distillation Attacks, Anthropic Warns
item.description: Anthropic accused DeepSeek, Moonshot and MiniMax of illicitly using Claude to steal some of the AI model’s capabilities

item.title: AI-powered Cyber-Attacks Up Significantly in the Last Year, Warns CrowdStrike
item.description: CrowdStrike Global Threat Report warns how adversaries are leveraging AI to make campaigns more efficient and more effective

item.title: Anthropic Says Chinese AI Firms Used 16 Million Claude Queries to Copy Model
item.description: Anthropic on Monday said it identified "industrial-scale campaigns" mounted by three artificial intelligence (AI) companies, DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax, to illegally extract Claude's capabilities to improve th...

item.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: The Evasive Adversary Wields AI
Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
item.description: The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.

item.title: APT28 Targeted European Entities Using Webhook-Based Macro Malware
item.description: The Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor tracked as APT28 has been attributed to a new campaign targeting specific entities in Western and Central Europe. The activity, per S2 Grupo's LAB52 threat intelligence t...

item.title: Shai-Hulud-Like Worm Targets Developers via npm and AI Tools
item.description: Supply chain worm mimicking Shai-Hulud malware spread via malicious npm packages, targeting AI tools has been identified by security researchers

item.title: Fraud Investigation Reveals Sophisticated Python Malware
item.description: Sophisticated Python malware uncovered in fraud probe shows obfuscation, disposable infrastructure

item.title: Another day, another malicious JPEG, (Mon, Feb 23rd)
item.description: In his last two diaries, Xavier discussed recent malware campaigns that download JPEG files with embedded malicious payload[1,2]. At that point in time, I've not come across the malicious â€œMSI imageâ€ myself, but wh...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Double-Tap Skimmers, PromptSpy AI, 30Tbps DDoS, Docker Malware & More
item.description: Security news rarely moves in a straight line. This week, it feels more like a series of sharp turns, some happening quietly in the background, others playing out in public view. The details are different, but the pre...

item.title: Russian Cyber Threat Actor Uses GenAI to Compromise Fortinet Firewalls
item.description: A low-skilled Russian-speaking attacker has used GenAI tools to help deploy a successful attack workflow targeting FortiGate instances

item.title: Leading Semiconductor Supplier Advantest Hit by Ransomware Attack
item.description: Advantest, a Japanese specialist in testing computer chips for major semiconductor manufacturers, has deployed incident response protocols following a cybersecurity incident

item.title: University of Mississippi Medical Center Still Offline After Ransomware Attack
item.description: University of Mississippi Medical Center is still scrambling to respond to a ransomware attack last Thursday

item.title: MuddyWater Targets MENA Organizations with GhostFetch, CHAR, and HTTP_VIP
item.description: The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater (aka Earth Vetala, Mango Sandstorm, and MUDDYCOAST) has targeted several organizations and individuals mainly located across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region...

item.title: Cloudflare One is the first SASE offering modern post-quantum encryption across the full platform
item.description: We’ve upgraded Cloudflare One to support post-quantum encryption by implementing the latest IETF drafts for hybrid ML-KEM into our Cloudflare IPsec product. This extends post-quantum encryption across all major Cloudf...

item.title: AI-Assisted Threat Actor Compromises 600+ FortiGate Devices in 55 Countries
item.description: A Russian-speaking, financially motivated threat actor has been observed taking advantage of commercial generative artificial intelligence (AI) services to compromise over 600 FortiGate devices located in 55 countries...

item.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Code Security for AI-Powered Vulnerability Scanning
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) company Anthropic has begun to roll out a new security feature for Claude Code that can scan a user's software codebase for vulnerabilities and suggest patches. The capability, called Clau...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Actively Exploited Roundcube Flaws to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added two security flaws impacting Roundcube webmail software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active...

item.title: EC-Council Expands AI Certification Portfolio to Strengthen U.S. AI Workforce Readiness and Security
item.description: With $5.5 trillion in global AI risk exposure and 700,000 U.S. workers needing reskilling, four new AI certifications and Certified CISO v4 help close the gap between AI adoption and workforce readiness. EC-Council, c...

item.title: Cloudflare outage on February 20, 2026
item.description: Cloudflare suffered a service outage on February 20, 2026. A subset of customers who use Cloudflare’s Bring Your Own IP (BYOIP) service saw their routes to the Internet withdrawn via Border Gateway Protocol (BGP).

item.title: AI-augmented threat actor accesses FortiGate devices at scale
item.description: Commercial AI services are enabling even unsophisticated threat actors to conduct cyberattacks at scale—a trend Amazon Threat Intelligence has been tracking closely. A recent investigation illustrates this shift: Amaz...

item.title: Cline CLI 2.3.0 Supply Chain Attack Installed OpenClaw on Developer Systems
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, the open-source, artificial intelligence (AI)-powered coding assistant Cline CLI was updated to stealthily install OpenClaw, a self-hosted autonomous AI agent that has beco...

item.title: Code Mode: give agents an entire API in 1,000 tokens
item.description: The Cloudflare API has over 2,500 endpoints. Exposing each one as an MCP tool would consume over 2 million tokens. With Code Mode, we collapsed all of it into two tools and roughly 1,000 tokens of context.

item.title: Latin America's Cyber Maturity Lags Threat Landscape
item.description: The slower pace of upgrades has the unintended impact of creating a haven for attackers, especially for initial access brokers and ransomware gangs.

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-49113 RoundCube Webmail Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability...

item.title: ClickFix Campaign Abuses Compromised Sites to Deploy MIMICRAT Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ClickFix campaign that abuses compromised legitimate sites to deliver a previously undocumented remote access trojan (RAT) called MIMICRAT (aka AstarionRAT). "...

item.title: Android Malware Hijacks Google Gemini to Stay Hidden
item.description: A new Android malware implant using Google Gemini to perform persistence tasks was discovered on VirusTotal and analyzed by ESET

item.title: FBI Reports 1,900 ATM Jackpotting Incidents Since 2020, $20M Lost in 2025
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) has warned of an increase in ATM jackpotting incidents across the country, leading to losses of more than $20 million in 2025. The agency said 1,900 ATM jackpotting incid...

item.title: Emerging Chiplet Designs Spark Fresh Cybersecurity Challenges
item.description: As scaled-down circuits with limited functions redefine computing for AI systems and autonomous vehicles, their flexibility demands new approaches to safeguard critical infrastructure.

item.title: Abu Dhabi Finance Week Exposed VIP Passport Details
item.description: Unprotected cloud data sends the wrong signal at a time when the emirate's trying to attract investors and establish itself as a global financial center.

item.title: PromptSpy Android Malware Abuses Gemini AI to Automate Recent-Apps Persistence
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered what they say is the first Android malware that abuses Gemini, Google's generative artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, as part of its execution flow and achieves persistence...

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Red Card 2.0 Arrests 651 in African Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description: An international cybercrime operation against online scams has led to 651 arrests and recovered more than $4.3 million as part of an effort led by law enforcement agencies from 16 African countries. The initiative, co...

item.title: Industrial-Scale Fake Coretax Apps Drive $2m Fraud in Indonesia
item.description: Fraud campaign exploiting Indonesia’s Coretax resulted in $1.5m to $2m in losses via malicious apps

item.title: Starkiller: New ‘Commercial-Grade’ Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
item.description: A new cybercriminal toolkit uses proxies to mimic popular online services and represents a “significant escalation in phishing infrastructure,” warn researchers at Abnormal

item.title: Flaws in Popular Software Development App Extensions Allow Data Exfiltration
item.description: Four serious new vulnerabilities affect Microsoft Visual Studio Code, Cursor and Windsurf extensions, three of which remain unpatched

item.title: Researchers Reveal Six New OpenClaw Vulnerabilities
item.description: Endor Labs has published details of six new vulnerabilities in popular AI assistant OpenClaw

item.title: More Than 40% of South Africans Were Scammed in 2025
item.description: Survey underscores the reality that scammers follow "scalable opportunities and low friction," rather than rich targets that tend to be better protected.

item.title: Dell's Hard-Coded Flaw: A Nation-State Goldmine
item.description: A China-related attacker has exploited the vendor flaw since mid-2024, allowing it to move laterally, maintain persistent access, and deploy malware.

item.title: A CISO's Playbook for Defending Data Assets Against AI Scraping
item.description: Discover a strategic approach to govern scraping risks, balance security with business growth, and safeguard intellectual capital from automated data harvesting.

item.title: Cryptojacking Campaign Exploits Driver to Boost Monero Mining
item.description: Cryptojacking campaign used pirated software to deploy a persistent XMRig miner with stealth tactics

item.title: AI Assistants Used as Covert Command-and-Control Relays
item.description: AIs like Grok and Microsoft Copilot can be exploited as covert C2 channels for malware communication

item.title: Record Number of Ransomware Victims and Groups in 2025
item.description: Searchlight Cyber reports a 30% annual increase in ransomware victim numbers in 2025

item.title: Chinese APT Group Exploits Dell Zero-Day for Two Years
item.description: Mandiant reveals campaign featuring exploit of a CVSS 10.0 CVE in Dell RecoverPoint for Virtual Machines

item.title: Android 17 Beta Introduces Secure-By-Default Architecture
item.description: Android 17 Beta introduces privacy, security updates and a new Canary channel for improved development

item.title: Low-Skilled Cybercriminals Use AI to Perform "Vibe Extortion" Attacks
item.description: Unit 42 researchers observed a low-skilled threat actor using an LLM to script a professional extortion strategy, complete with deadlines and pressure tactics

item.title: Over-Privileged AI Drives 4.5 Times Higher Incident Rates
item.description: Teleport study reveals that organizations running over-privileged AI have a 76% incident rate

item.title: Significant Rise in Ransomware Attacks Targeting Industrial Operations
item.description: Dragos annual report warns of a surge in ransomware attacks causing increased operational disruption in industrial environments

item.title: Building an AI-powered defense-in-depth security architecture for serverless microservices
item.description: Enterprise customers face an unprecedented security landscape where sophisticated cyber threats use artificial intelligence to identify vulnerabilities, automate attacks, and evade detection at machine speed. Traditio...

item.title: Operation DoppelBrand: Weaponizing Fortune 500 Brands
item.description: The GS7 cyber-threat group targets US financial institutions with near-perfect imitations of corporate portals to steal credentials and gain remote access.

item.title: Vulnerabilities in Password Managers Allow Hackers to View and Change Passwords
item.description: Security researchers have challenged end-to-end encryption claims from popular commercial password managers

item.title: OysterLoader Evolves With New C2 Infrastructure and Obfuscation
item.description: OysterLoader malware evolves into 2026, refining C2 infrastructure, obfuscation & infection stages

item.title: Operation DoppelBrand Weaponizes Trusted Brands For Credential Theft
item.description: New phishing campaign dubbed Operation DoppelBrand targeted major financial firms like Wells Fargo

item.title: 260K+ Chrome Users Duped by Fake AI Browser Extensions
item.description: 30 copycat apps tricked users, and Google itself, into thinking they're legitimate AI tools.

item.title: Google Warns of In the Wild Exploit as It Patches New Chrome Zero Day
item.description: A high severity vulnerability in Google Chrome and allows remote attackers to execute code

item.title: Crypto Payments to Human Traffickers Surges 85%
item.description: Chainalysis warns that online fraud is fuelling sophisticated human trafficking operations

item.title: Odido Breach Impacts Millions of Dutch Telco Users
item.description: Dutch telco Odido has revealed a major data breach impacting over six million customers

item.title: Munich Security Conference: Cyber Threats Lead G7 Risk Index, Disinformation Ranks Third
item.description: G7 countries ranked cyber-attacks as the top risk, while BICS members placed cyber threats only as the eighth most pressing risk

item.title: Fake AI Assistants in Google Chrome Web Store Steal Passwords and Spy on Emails
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of users have downloaded malicious AI extensions masquerading as ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok and others, warn cybersecurity researchers at LayerX

item.title: Ivanti EPMM Zero-Day Bugs Spark Exploit Frenzy — Again
item.description: It's time to phase out the "patch and pray" approach, eliminate needless public interfaces, and enforce authentication controls, one expert says.

item.title: Booz Allen Announces General Availability of Vellox Reverser to Automate Malware Defense
item.description: The AI-powered product delivers expert-grade malware analysis and reverse engineering in minutes.

item.title: Introducing Markdown for Agents
item.description: The way content is discovered online is shifting, from traditional search engines to AI agents that need structured data from a Web built for humans. It’s time to consider not just human visitors, but start to treat a...

item.title: World Leaks Ransomware Group Adds Stealthy, Custom Malware ‘RustyRocket’ to Attacks
item.description: Accenture Cybersecurity warns over difficult to detect, “sophisticated toolset” being deployed as part of extortion campaigns

item.title: Nation-State Hackers Embrace Gemini AI for Malicious Campaigns, Google Finds
item.description: Google researchers found that government-backed hackers now use AI throughout the whole attack lifecycle

item.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2024-43468 Microsoft Configuration Manager SQL Injection Vulnerability CVE-2...

item.title: AI Skills Represent Dangerous New Attack Surface, Says TrendAI
item.description: New TrendAI report warns that most security tools can’t protect against attacks on AI skills artifacts

item.title: Time to Exploit Plummets as N-Day Flaws Dominate
item.description: Flashpoint warns of a dramatic drop in the average time between vulnerability disclosure and exploitation

item.title: Nation-State Actors Exploit Notepad++ Supply Chain
item.description: Unit 42 reveals new infrastructure associated with the Notepad++ attack. This expands understanding of threat actor operations and malware delivery. The post Nation-State Actors Exploit Notepad++ Supply Chain appeared...

item.title: Social Media Platforms Earn Billions from Scam Ads
item.description: Revolut claims social media sites make £3.8bn annually from scam ads targeting European users

item.title: Researchers Find 40,000+ Exposed OpenClaw Instances
item.description: SecurityScorecard has identified over 40,000 OpenClaw deployments exposed to potential attack

item.title: US Agencies Told to Scrap End of Support Edge Devices
item.description: CISA has issued a new directive requiring federal agencies to decommission all end of support edge devices within 12 months to reduce ongoing exploitation risks

item.title: Novel Technique to Detect Cloud Threat Actor Operations
item.description: We introduce a novel method that maps cloud alert trends to MITRE ATT&CK techniques. The patterns created could identify threat actors by behavior. The post Novel Technique to Detect Cloud Threat Actor Operations appe...
